summary: Prasinezumab has not been studied or shown to be effective for Parkinson’s disease; current literature does not support its use due to its primary focus on Alzheimer’s rather than Parkinson’s pathology. The available information confirms that there are no clinically validated trials demonstrating prasinezumab’s benefit in treating symptoms or progression of Parkinson’s disease.
